macro_segments.title: Introduction to Perek HaMocher et HaSpina: Sale of a Boat and Its Appurtenances

micro_segments.title: Opening of the new perek and overview of the sale of boats
Mishnah: What is included when selling a boat
Distinction between included items (mast, sails, anchors, oars) and excluded items (slaves, cargo, sacks)
Exception clause: kol mah shebetochah (everything in it) includes even slaves and cargo

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Detailed Definitions of Boat Components and Related Items

micro_segments.title: Definition of toran (mast) with biblical sources
Definition of nes (sails) with biblical sources
Definition of ugin (anchors) with biblical sources and etymology
Definition of manhigin (oars) with biblical sources
Baraita: Additional items included with boat (iskala, bor hamayim)

macro_segments.title: Transition and Rashbam's Conceptual Framework: Chattel vs. Land and Gift/Hekdesh Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Explanation of why movables are sold with boats but not with land
Rashbam's conceptual framework: relationship between this perek and previous discussion of hekdesh
Detailed reading of Rashbam: why gifts and hekdesh include more with land than with chattel

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Detailed Definitions of Boat Components and Related Items (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Natan and Sumchus: Disputes regarding dinghy (betzi/dugit)
Geographical and linguistic explanation of regional boat terminology

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Section: Seafarers' Tales of Extraordinary Waves and Divine Protection

micro_segments.title: Introduction to aggada: Rabbah reports stories from seafarers
Story of the drowning wave with divine names carved on rods for salvation
Rambam and other rishonim on the interpretation of aggada: literal vs. metaphorical
Maharsha's interpretation: waves as non-Jewish nations, God's name as protection
HaGra's interpretation: waves as yetzer hara, internal spiritual struggle

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Massive Waves Separated by Vast Distances

micro_segments.title: Description of the distance between waves (300 parsa) and height of individual waves
Narrative of sailors lifted by waves to the height of stars
Dialogue between two waves about their destructive power and limitations
Wave's explanation of sand as God's boundary for the ocean

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: Hormin Bar Lilit the Demon's Feats

micro_segments.title: Rabbah's sighting of the demon Hormin bar Lilit running on the wall of Machuza
Hormin's acrobatic feat: jumping between mules while holding wine cups
Explanation of Hormin as half-demon (offspring of human and demoness) and his eventual death
Student interpretation: Hormin as yetzer hara and self-indulgence leading to barrenness

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Enormous Day-Old Antelope

micro_segments.title: Description of an antelope (re'em) just one day old the size of Mount Tabor
Measurements of the antelope: neck length and head resting place
The antelope's dung blocking the Jordan River

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Chain of Consumption (Frog, Snake, Raven, Tree)

micro_segments.title: Series of creatures: frog swallowed by snake, snake by raven, raven lands on tree
Question of the tree's strength supporting all these creatures
Maharsha's interpretation: talmidei chachamim as frogs, nations as predators, Torah as supporting tree

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Massive Fish and Its Multiple Uses

micro_segments.title: The fish with parasite in its nostrils, death, and washing ashore destroying 60 cities
Usage of the fish: eaten by 60 cities, salted for 60 more, oil from eye, bones for rebuilding
Interpretation: fish death as death of righteous person, town reactions as three classes

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Deceptive Fish Island

micro_segments.title: Sailors mistake fish for island due to sand and grass growing on its back
Sailors camp, cook, and bake on the fish; it overturns when heated
Interpretation: fish as wicked nations who befriend then betray; boat as divine help

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Multi-Day Journey Along the Fish

micro_segments.title: Boat traveling between fins of a fish for three days and three nights
Fish going against wind while boat has wind assistance yet still takes three days
Rav Dimi: the boat's speed is 60 parsa in the time to heat a kettle
Fish is faster than an arrow shot by a horseman
Interpretation: other nations live in homelands like fish in water; Jews always in exile moving against wind
Rav Ashi: the fish is identified as Gildana with two special fins

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Bird with Feet in Water and Head in Sky

micro_segments.title: Description of a bird: feet in water up to ankles, head reaching the sky
Sailors think the water is shallow due to bird's ankle depth
Heavenly voice warns of danger: axe fell for seven years without hitting bottom
Explanation: water is not deep but current is dangerously strong
Interpretation: bird as Jewish people with feet grounded in reality but heads in heavens; danger as esoteric studies
Alternative interpretation: warning against enjoying physical pleasures of other nations

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Fat Geese and Messianic Punishment

micro_segments.title: Description of geese so fat their feathers fall off; river of fat flows beneath them
Rabbah asks geese if he can benefit from them in the world to come
Geese respond affirmatively by lifting wing and leg
Rabbi Elazar: Jews will be held accountable for these geese
Rashbam interpretation: geese are created for Messianic times; delay of Messiah causes their suffering; Jews will pay

macro_segments.title: Aggadic Story: The Arab Tracker and Sand-Smelling Navigation

micro_segments.title: Arab itinerant travels with group, able to determine direction by smelling sand
Group tests the Arab by giving him sand from other locations
Arab accurately determines distances: eight parsa, three parsa, despite reversal and deception
Conclusion: group cannot disprove Arab's tracking ability; similar real trackers exist

topical_tags.term: Boat and ship terminology (toran, nes, ugin, manhigin)
Aggadata (non-legal interpretations) and its interpretation methods
Divine names and protection (Ehyeh Asher Ehyeh, Hashem, etc.)
Fish and sea creatures (kavra, tanina, gildana)
Torah as tree of life and support
Jewish people in exile vs. other nations
Yetzer hara and internal spiritual struggle
Rabbah and Rabbah bar bar Chana (distinct sages)
Messianic times and delay of Messiah
